title: kaori.pipeline.silver.complete
description:
Fired when Silver layer is ready (post-cleaning OR pre-analysis trigger). Two producer sites today emit slightly different optional fields — see ``Required vs optional`` in README. Required surface = what the consumer (ai-orchestrator/consumers/pipeline_consumer.py:_handle_silver_complete) actually reads.

Follow-up cleanup task: split into kaori.pipeline.silver.cleaned (clean.py emits) and kaori.pipeline.silver.queued_for_analysis (analyze.py emits) so each producer owns a single shape. Tracked separately — this schema captures today's reality.

run_id.description: pipeline_runs.run_id — UUID string. Consumer joins on this to find queued analysis_runs.

enterprise_id.description: Tenant scope. Consumer uses this for acquire_for_tenant before reading analysis_runs.

row_count.description: Optional — set by clean.py after silver_rows insert. Not used by the orchestrator consumer; reserved for the gold consumer's stats.

analysis_run_id.description: Optional — set by analyze.py when an explicit POST /analytics/runs created the analysis_run row. Consumer falls back to looking up queued runs by (run_id, enterprise_id) when absent.

consent_external_ai.description: Optional — set by analyze.py to forward the K-4 consent flag to the runner without an extra DB lookup.
